summary: Issue tracking built for modern software teams.
editor_note: Someone finally rebuilt project management for teams that live on the keyboard. Keyboard shortcuts, a stripped-back issue flow, and an interface that stays out of the way make it feel closer to an editor than Jira. In a market full of feature-bloated software, Linear chose speed and restraint. That trade only works if you are willing to say no more often than your competitors.
why_featured: Defined a new standard for dev tools

product_evidence.claim: Official product positioning describes Linear: Issue tracking built for modern software teams.
Someone finally rebuilt project management for teams that live on the keyboard.

market_context.primary_user: Developers and engineering teams evaluating build, deployment, or infrastructure workflows.
market_context.differentiation: Defined a new standard for dev tools
market_context.why_now: Recent source-backed funding and public product evidence make project management, issue tracking, developer tools worth tracking.

risks.claim: Continued tracking should verify whether the public product evidence turns into durable customer adoption beyond the currently visible surface.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
